evidence: currentContextId IS updated on every stt.audio packet (index.ts:165-167: `if (audioPkt.contextId) { this.currentContextId = audioPkt.contextId; }`). But the provider-final read site does NOT blindly use it. handleProviderMessage (line 220-222): `const providerContextId = msg["is_final"] === true ? this.contextIdForProviderFinal({ speechFinal, fromFinalize }) : this.currentContextId;`. contextIdForProviderFinal (line 473-477) anchors turn-completing finals to the oldest PENDING finalize context, not currentContextId: `const pending = this.pendingProviderFinalizeContextIds[0]; if (pending && (flags.speechFinal || flags.fromFinalize)) return pending; return this.currentContextId;`. The exact reordering case kimi describes is covered by two passing tests: 'correlates a pending Finalize response to the requested context across turn.change' (index.test.ts:662) sends audio for turn-old, fires turn.change to turn-new (advancing currentContextId), then a late from_finalize is_final arrives and is correctly attributed to turn-old; and 'keeps Finalize response correlation after already released final text crosses turn.change' (index.test.ts:732) proves the same across a released-final boundary. Provider messages do arrive on socket I/O (onMessage, line 151-153) independent of the bus drain loop, so the interleaving window is real — but the consequential finals (the ones that emit eos.turn_complete and feed the LLM, line 266-271 / pushTurnComplete) are anchored via the pending-finalize queue, not currentContextId.
real_fix: No change to the eos/turn-completing path — the correlation guard (contextIdForProviderFinal + pendingProviderFinalizeContextIds) already prevents misattribution of speechFinal/from_finalize finals, and is regression-tested. Kimi's suggested fix (only update currentContextId on turn-boundary events) is wrong for this design: currentContextId is intentionally the live-audio cursor used for sendAudio routing (line 168), audio-byte stats (line 170), and interim-display attribution (pushInterim line 273); decoupling it from audio would break interim routing. The only residual exposure is a plain is_final with speech_final:false AND no outstanding finalize (a mid-utterance interim-final from Deepgram's own endpointing) hitting the line-222 fallback after a barge-in advanced the context — this affects only an interim stt.result/stt.interim segment, never a turn boundary, and Deepgram emits such segments near-synchronously rather than seconds-late across a turn change, so blast radius is cosmetic. If hardening is desired, attribute non-speechFinal is_final segments to currentContextId at packet-arrival time captured against the most-recent NON-pending audio context, or skip the fallback when finalizedContextIds already advanced — but this is a low-priority refinement, not a medium defect.
